text.title: Kill Or Keep Criteria Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Kill Or Keep Criteria application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Verdict MUST score all four floors (MRR / traffic / joy / opportunity-cost); partial scoring is rejected.
rule.rationale: Single-floor verdicts overweight one signal (usually MRR) and ignore why-it-exists.

rule.statement: Every floor score MUST cite verbatim evidence (URL, ticket id, dashboard snapshot timestamp); free-floating opinions are rejected.
rule.rationale: Anti-hallucination: LLMs invent plausible scores; verbatim anchors make the artefact falsifiable.

rule.statement: Output MUST be a single binary verdict (kill | keep); 'pause' / 'maybe' / 'monitor' are rejected — those collapse to keep-forever.
rule.rationale: Soft verdicts drift into perpetual keep; the binary forces commitment.

rule.statement: Verdict MUST carry the next concrete action (sunset announcement draft, next-review date, refund window).
Each filled instance records template_version + last_reviewed; un-versioned instances are rejected.
rule.rationale: Schema-evolution practice — old instances must remain re-parseable.
